languageModal.description: This modal allows you to save your language choice to translate the site.

bam.brasserie: Since 2009, we have been brewing beer in Montflours in Mayenne (53). First in the field of amateur brewing, then in real economic activity with two employees. Whether it is top or bottom fermentation, mono- or multi-step brewing, blondes, ambers or browns come out of the BAM tanks. A production today of 400 hl/year between recipes that have proven themselves and experiments, but always based on raw materials from organic farming. The Nature and Progress label reinforces this commitment, in line also with the social values that BAM intends to defend.
bam.associative: BAM has always wanted to experiment, learn, and share knowledge about brewing. Based on this desire, a collective dynamic was launched, initially in the form of an association, later transitioning to a SCIC, a cooperative society of collective interest, in 2013. Comprising 98 members, with a steering committee and a manager who report annually on the proper conduct of the project at the general assembly.
bam.montflours: BAM's objective has always been the production and sale of organic beers in Mayenne. The first brews took place in Montflours (250 inhabitants) and BAM is still located there. Participating in local dynamics is a leitmotif. For the most part of BAM's recipes, the barley is produced by Mayenne farmers and the hops come from the region.

services.title: Our services
services.formation: The best way to understand beer making is to make it! We offer a small-scale beer brewing workshop. During this day in our premises, you brew your own beer, take the fermenter home to monitor fermentation, and return for bottling 15 days later. Workshop on Saturdays from 10 AM to 4:30 PM by reservation. Price: €200 for 2 people + €35/person additional
services.commande: We offer kegs in 20 and 30 L for your events: blonde and amber all year round and other beers on order. Free loan of a tap and cups, by reservation. Return of non-perforated kegs.

urgence.textBlock: You can find our products in the various points of sale listed below on the map. We also sell directly at the Montflours market (5:30 PM to 7 PM) and Argentré market (5 PM to 7:30 PM). For orders, feel free to contact us. A BAM emergency? Don't hesitate to contact us by clicking here:

history.title: The BAM Story in a Few Words

texte.slide1: 2009 - The Origins: In Montflours, a group of friends tries their hand at home brewing in a kitchen.
texte.slide2: 2010 - The Pico-Brewery: The Brasserie Associative de Montflours association is created, with about fifteen members. Members train in brewery setup and social utility. Recipes and tests follow one after another, and the idea of a SCIC (cooperative society of collective interest) takes root, with the goal of producing and selling organic beers in Mayenne.
texte.slide3: 2012 – Launch of the activity: The project becomes a microbrewery thanks to European Union aid. It's the time of firsts: first employment of a brewer for Cédric and first brewing room in a farm building in Mezoué; first sales, first labels, but also first local animation with the impulse of the Au Champ market, Friday evening in Montflours.
texte.slide4: 2013 New direction: purchase of new fermentation tanks, relocation of the brewery to the milking room of the Fougeray farm, first brews in a converted milk tank, Nature et Progrès certification, and above all, transition to a SCIC. BAM, then consisting of 83 members, sets its missions as social utility and collective interest around the rural territory, popular education, conviviality, and experimental and artisanal brewing.
texte.slide5: 2015 Over the seasons, BAM further establishes the brewing sector in the Montflours setting, acquiring 80 hop plants.
texte.slide6: 2016 A new brewing room (twice as large as the previous one) with a fermenter is purchased.
texte.slide7: 2017 A second brewer, Pierre, is hired.
texte.slide8: 2019 A deposit system is implemented and 90% of local barley is used for brewing.
texte.slide9:
2020 – Integration into L’Archipel: The brewery initiates the creation of a shared building (bringing together several local artisans in the form of a SCIC, L’Archipel) in the local industrial area and moves there in September 2020.
 And the adventure continues!

slide1.title: The Origins
slide1.text: In Montflours, a group of friends tries their hand at home brewing in a kitchen.

slide2.title: The Pico-Brewery
slide2.text: The Brasserie Associative de Montflours association is created, with about fifteen members. Members train in brewery setup and social utility. Recipes and tests follow one after another, and the idea of a SCIC (cooperative society of collective interest) takes root, with the goal of producing and selling organic beers in Mayenne.

slide3.title: Launch of the Activity
slide3.text: The project becomes a microbrewery thanks to European Union aid. It's the time of firsts: first employment of a brewer for Cédric and first brewing room in a farm building in Mezoué; first sales, first labels, but also first local animation with the impulse of the Au Champ market, Friday evening in Montflours.

slide4.title: New Direction
slide4.text: Purchase of new fermentation tanks, relocation of the brewery to the milking room of the Fougeray farm, first brews in a converted milk tank, Nature et Progrès certification, and above all, transition to a SCIC. BAM, then consisting of 83 members, sets its missions as social utility and collective interest around the rural territory, popular education, conviviality, and experimental and artisanal brewing.

slide5.title: Over the Seasons
slide5.text: BAM further establishes the brewing sector in the Montflours setting, acquiring 80 hop plants.

slide6.title: New Brewing Room
slide6.text: A new brewing room (twice as large as the previous one) with a fermenter is purchased.

slide7.title: Second Brewer
slide7.text: A second brewer, Pierre, is hired.

slide8.title: Deposit System
slide8.text: A deposit system is implemented and 90% of local barley is used for brewing.

slide9.title: Mooring at L'Archipel
slide9.text: The brewery initiates the creation of a shared building (bringing together several local artisans in the form of a SCIC, L'Archipel) in the local industrial area and moves there in September 2020. And the adventure continues!

nature.title: BAM is diuretic and good for the planet!!

content.paragraph1: We are in agreement with many Great Planetary Issues and work on local responses specific to our activity:
list: Short supply chains (in purchase and sale: towards 100% Mayenne)
Zero waste (recovery of packaging in good condition, return of 75 cl and 33 cl bottles and reuse)
Cooperation with other breweries and other companies.
Popular education: training, visits, events, knowledge transfer,...
content.paragraph2: The Nature et Progrès label validates our approach and helps us progress. Everything is well presented below:
